stories.title: Ukraine leads European efforts to counter Russia’s growing ballistic missile threat
stories.summary: As the Russian army struggles to advance in Ukraine, Vladimir Putin is increasingly relying on ballistic missiles to maintain the momentum of his faltering invasion. While Ukraine’s air defenses have dramatically improved since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022, the only platform in Kyiv’s arsenal that is capable of consistently intercepting ballistic missiles is the US-made Patriot system. However, due to limited US production and growing demand for interceptors in the Middle East, there is simply not enough ammunition available to protect Ukraine’s cities and civilian infrastructure. Putin is now taking full advantage of this shortage.

stories.title: Road to Europe: How Ajax can qualify for the UEFA Conference League
stories.summary: It's an unusual sight, but Ajax are forced to enter Europe through the qualifying rounds of the UEFA Conference League. Flashscore takes you through the whole route of the Dutch giants. It was a terrible campaign for Ajax : a joint-worst points finish since 1963 forced them to appear in the Eredivisie play-offs for a ticket for the UEFA Conference League qualifiers. The Amsterdam side, then managed by Spaniard Oscar Garcia, survived, beating FC Groningen 2-0 and FC Utrecht on penalties in Volendam, where Ajax were forced to play due to Harry Styles' 10-concert residence in the Johan Cruyff ArenA.

stories.title: Italy soccer federation speaks with Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti for coaching role
stories.summary: Open this photo in gallery: Manchester City's head coach Pep Guardiola applauds the fans after his last game with the club after a Premier League match against Aston Villa in Manchester, England, in May. Alastair Grant/The Associated Press The Italian soccer federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup.

stories.title: Love Island fans are convinced Maya Jama gave away her real opinion on Islander
stories.summary: Love Island viewers are convinced that Maya Jama isn't Aidan's biggest fan after spotting what they believe was a telling moment during the latest episode of the ITV2 series. This Thursday's (23 July) episode saw the Islanders take part in another instalment of Not Another Podcast, where they were grilled over their connections. With the 2026 series nearing its conclusion, some couples appeared stronger than ever as Maya probed them, encouraging them to reflect on their time in Mallorca and reveal behind-the-scenes secrets. But the gossip wasn't the only thing that kept viewers talking.

stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor
stories.summary:
Rudakubana is serving a 52-year sentence after the fatal stabbing of three girls and the attempted murder of eight other children and two adults at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport, Merseyside, in July 2024.

The killer, who was 17 at the time of the attack, pleaded guilty to killing Alice da Silva Aguiar, nine, Bebe King, six, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven.

Rudakabana was transferred from the category A prison to Broadmoor high-security hospital on Thursday in a move that does not change his sentence, the MoJ said.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: A24 Plans Free Screenings of Anthony Bourdain Movie ‘Tony’ for Hospitality Workers (Exclusive)
stories.summary: Skip to main content Home Movies Movie News The “Industry Night” screenings will take place in partnership with OpenTable and Square across 25 cities, with all screenings hosted by local chefs. July 23, 2026 12:00pm Antonio Banderas (left) and Dominic Sessa in 'Tony. ' Courtesy of A24 Ahead of the release of the anticipated Anthony Bourdain movie, Tony, A24 has set a series of free advanced screenings for hospitality and food service workers. In partnership with OpenTable and Square, the screenings are set up for servers, dishwashers, line cooks, maître d’s and beyond. Dubbed “Industry Night”, the advanced screenings will take place on Aug.
sources: Bon Appétit: Recipes, Cooking, Entertaining, Restaurants | Bon Appétit

stories.title: Cole Hauser admits Taylor Sheridan's absence from writers' room created 'challenges' on 'Dutton Ranch'
stories.summary: Cole Hauser is giving fans a glimpse behind the scenes of "Dutton Ranch" — and it wasn't all smooth riding. The actor, who reprises his fan-favorite role as Rip Wheeler in the "Yellowstone" spin-off, acknowledged the cast faced an uphill battle after franchise creator Taylor Sheridan stepped away from the show's writers' room during the spin-off’s first season. "There were challenges, you know? " Hauser told Film Inside NYC. "Not having Taylor [Sheridan] there, for us to really kind of be able to work with these new writers, these new directors, these people. Everything had changed.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of beating Arsenal to £100m-rated Bradley Barcola transfer
stories.summary: LIVERPOOL are confident of beating Arsenal to £100million-rated Bradley Barcola. The Gunners are eyeing the Paris St Germain and France ace after missing out to Chelsea on the signing of £117m Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. Bradkey Barcola is in-demand this summer Credit: Getty Morgan Rogers signed for Chelsea in a £117m deal Credit: Getty But SunSport understands Liverpool are in the driving seat for Barcola , who is also wanted by Bayern Munich . The Reds are on the hunt for a marquee forward signing after the departure of veteran Mo Salah . Sign up for the Arsenal newsletter Thank you!

stories.title: Merry Christmas in July
stories.summary: Looking around some of the stores and online shopping sites, we find it is time for another celebration — Christmas in July. Not everyone will have a Christmas in July celebration, because I have heard some say it takes away from our true Christmas celebration in December. I see it a different way. Everyone needs a bit of encouragement from time to time, and Christmas in July gives us that opportunity. Not only can we remind people that we love them, but with a little bit of effort we can remind them of the real reason we will celebrate Christmas in 22 weeks and 2 days!

stories.title: Final hurrah? Glasgow’s cut-price Commonwealth Games fight for survival
stories.summary: S ometimes when a non-responsive person is close to death, a strange medical phenomena occurs. From apparently nowhere they experience a burst of energy. They recognise people again. They start to speak in sentences. Relatives even dare to hope. But, sadly, that hope is short-lived. Doctors call it terminal lucidity. And it comes to mind at the dawn of the 23rd Commonwealth Games . Less than three years ago, the Games were on life support after the original hosts, Victoria, effectively paid AUS$380m (£199m) to pull out because it feared losses running into the billions. Glasgow then stepped in to host a cheaper and stripped back event, with only 10 sports compared to 19 four years ago.

stories.title: Ajax begin the 2026/2027 season with an impressive victory over FK Vojvodina
stories.summary: Ajax played their first official match of the 2026/2027 season against FK Vojvodina on Thursday. The Amsterdam side won the second qualifying round fixture for the UEFA Conference League group stage 1-4. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Davy Klaassen gave his team the lead as early as the third minute. Ajax therefore made a strong start to their first official match under new head coach Míchel Sánchez. The Spanish manager was making his official debut as Ajax head coach. For almost half an hour, the Amsterdam side dominated the match in Novi Sad. Petar Sukacev's equaliser caused a brief shift in momentum.

stories.title: Volodymyr Zelensky's firing of defence minister criticised as 'own goal'
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has been championed the world over for his wartime leadership, even at times being compared to Winston Churchill. But a decision to fire his popular, tech-savvy 35-year-old Defence Minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, last week baffled many. There were reports Fedorov was clashing with Ukraine's Soviet-era military chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. The younger man was fired. It drew criticism from former military generals, Western allies and analysts who described the move as an ill-judged mistake, particularly at a time when Ukraine was making gains on the battlefield thanks largely to innovation. The decision was also deeply unpopular with his own people.

stories.title: Highlights FK Vojvodina - Ajax | Abdalla puts finishing touch on victory
stories.summary: Ajax have taken a strong advantage into the return leg of their UEFA Conference League tie against FK Vojvodina at the Johan Cruijff ArenA. The first leg in Serbia ended in a convincing 1-4 win for the Ajax side. Debutant Mohamed Abdalla scored Ajax's fourth goal of the evening, putting the finishing touch on an impressive victory. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles In conversation with Míchel Sánchez about his philosophy, the squad and FK Vojvodina Ahead of Ajax's first competitive fixture of the season, away to FK Vojvodina, we sat down for an in-depth conversation with head coach Míchel Sánchez. How did he experienced his first few weeks at Ajax?

stories.title: Chandrashekhar Azad, Bal Gangadhar Tilak Jayanti celebrated
stories.summary: Madanapalle: The birth anniversary of freedom fighters Chandrashekhar Azad and Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak was celebrated on Thursday at Muthu English Medium School, run by Chaitanya Voluntary Organisation. Principal A Kavitha Rani highlighted the sacrifices, patriotism, and leadership of the two legends of Indian Independence Movement and urged students to draw inspiration from their lives. Students presented patriotic songs and speeches on the occasion. Teachers Janardhan Reddy, Prasanna, Netra, Bharathi, Bhargavi, other staff and students participated in the programme.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: ECB reveals shortlisted designs for new banknotes and launches public survey
stories.summary: PRESS RELEASE 23 July 2026 ECB unveils ten shortlisted design proposals for next series of euro banknotes Europeans invited to have their say in online survey open until 21 September Governing Council expected to select one design proposal around the end of the year The European Central Bank (ECB) today unveiled the shortlisted design proposals for the next series of euro banknotes and launched a public survey inviting people across Europe to provide their feedback. These design proposals are based on two different themes – “European culture” and “Rivers and birds” – and on the associated motifs chosen to illustrate them.

stories.title: VfB Stuttgart eyeing Man United goalkeeper Radek Vitek
stories.summary: VfB Stuttgart are reportedly considering a move for Manchester United’s Radek Vitek amidst their goalkeeping crisis. United exit A loan move to Bristol City in the 2025/26 campaign proved transformative for Vitek, who delivered superbly and accelerated his growth considerably. Initially signed to be a back-up shot-stopper, it didn’t take him too long to become an undisputed starter. Vitek made 41 Championship appearances , keeping 12 clean sheets in that period. He won Bristol’s Player of the Year award after a fan vote, the Players’ Player of the Year award, and the Young Player of the Year award.

stories.title: Man dies of injuries after attack at Regensburg bank: police
stories.summary: Police said on Thursday that a man had died after being critically wounded in an apparent hostage situation at a bank branch in Regensburg, Bavaria. The man was taken to a local hospital but was pronounced dead soon after. A major police operation, including special units and a helicopter, had gathered near the scene on Thursday afternoon. What else do we know about the standoff? Police said they were investigating "a homicide as well as suspected attempted robbery," in an update later on Thursday. "Two people were still inside the building. Special units were able to bring them out of the building in the mean time," police said. At around 6 p. m.

stories.title: Raging Love Island fans brand Priya a ‘bully’ after vicious Julia row – as she lashes out again
stories.summary: Love Island fans are outraged as Priya continues to clash with Julia, accusing her of being a "bully" after they argued over Priya's relationship with Aidan.

stories.title: 'I made awkward comment to Bradley Walsh - he had perfect response'
stories.summary: A pub manager has disclosed what Bradley Walsh was like during his visit to her Wiltshire pub. While her colleagues instantly recognised the television personality, Erin Williams, who took over control of the pub last year, confessed she hadn't a clue who he was - and even informed The Chase presenter of this fact. Erin revealed that she was raised in South Africa and doesn't always recognise British celebrities, though her fellow team members identified him immediately. Speaking to the Gazette and Herald, she said punters and staff were pleased to see him, with the pub's social media coordinator Kate declaring: "Oh my God, can you believe he's here?

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Regional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”. The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Gordon Brown to put the government on a war footing during the 2008 financial crash, arguing that economic growth requires the same level of urgency, focus and collective action. In an article for the Guardian , he wrote: “When I entered Downing Street, I told staff that the era of fighting devolution is over. No one in government loses when power is shared.

stories.title: 'We contributed to our own downfall' - Higgins
stories.summary: Coleraine manager Ruaidhrí Higgins says his side "contributed" to their own downfall in Thursday's 5-0 away defeat by HJK in the first leg of their Uefa Conference League second qualifying round tie. The Bannsiders were two down at the break through goals from Liam Moller and Pyry Mentu, while Toivo Mero added a third early in the second have with a quickfire brace by Teemu Pukki completing the rout. Higgins expressed his "disappointment" at the manner of the defeat but had no complaints as they now have a mountain to climb in next week's second leg at home. "The manner of the goals we gave away and the timing were extremely disappointing," Higgins told Coleraine TV.

stories.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

stories.title: Odysseus Has Been Funko Pop-ed
stories.summary: If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission. In a sentence that would surely leave Homer dumbfounded, Odysseus has been Funko Pop-ed. The epic Greek hero has been miniaturized into the popular collectible following the blockbuster release of “ The Odyssey . ” The 5-inch figurine showcases a bearded Odysseus, played by Matt Damon in the Christopher Nolan adaptation, clad in a warrior helmet, cloak and belt, sword in hand. The new collectible is now available to pre-order on the Funko website and Amazon . NEW RELEASE Odysseus Funko Pop!

stories.title: Judge Extends Order Blocking Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger for Another 14 Days
stories.summary: A federal judge on Thursday extended the order blocking the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for an additional 14 days, meaning the deal cannot close before Aug. 18. Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin is scheduled to hold a hearing on Aug. 3 on a request for a preliminary injunction that would block the deal indefinitely. Paramount is asking for additional time to make its case against the injunction. A coalition of 12 state attorneys general alleges that the deal violates the Clayton Act because it would reduce competition in the basic cable and theatrical distribution markets.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The announcement followed visits by state and health ministers to the hospital where Wangchuk was treated. Meanwhile, the youth-led movement behind the protests, CJP, vows to continue demonstrations until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham will work from No 10 North in Manchester today, marking the first day of the new northern branch of Downing Street. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the site by the end of next year as part of a push to decentralise power from London. The move coincides with a National Economic Council meeting chaired by Burnham in Manchester.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR crushed local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. The Robins surged ahead with five tries, including a 95-metre intercept score, while Hull FC managed just four goals despite a strong start. The win lifts Hull KR into the play-offs as Hull FC suffer their sixth straight defeat.

stories.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
stories.summary: Amazon’s Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions, now connecting to smart home devices from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others. The update allows Alexa to automatically route requests like adjusting a washing machine’s cycle for a cold wash. Amazon plans to expand third-party integrations further using its new AI developer toolkit.

stories.title: Defoe leaves Woking after 'eye-opening' first managerial role
stories.summary: Former England and Tottenham striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his departure from National League side Woking after just four months and nine matches in charge. Former England striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his exit from National League side Woking after just four months and six league games in charge. The 43-year-old was named the replacement for Neal Ardley in March and won two of those six matches to guide the club to a 10th-placed finish last season.

stories.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
stories.summary: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319. 99 (usually $399. 99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. [Product: Amazon Echo Show 21 | https://bestbuy. 7tiv. net/c/482924/614286/10014? u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww. bestbuy. com%2Fproduct%2Famazon-echo-show-21-full-hd-21-smart-display-for-home-organization-with-built-in-fire-tv-and-alexa-black%2FJ39HW6R5LZ, https://go. skimresources. com/?

stories.title: High expenses cloud Tesla's balance sheet: stock falls sharply
stories.summary: Tesla shares plummeted by more than a seventh on Thursday. The reason is that Tesla is spending many times more than the business is earning. Furthermore, general costs have risen by almost half. Even the revenue growth of a good quarter could not prevent operating profit from falling. In the three months of April, May, and June 2026, Tesla delivered more electric cars . Revenue from this division increased by 23 percent to 20. 5 billion US dollars compared to Tesla's second quarter of 2025. With batteries and solar cells, the company generated 3. 1 billion US dollars in revenue (+13%). Revenue from car repairs and other services increased particularly sharply, by 50 percent to 4.

stories.title: Pruett Riding Momentum, Hagan Looking for Repeat Win in Seattle at NHRA Northwest Nationals
stories.summary: The Muckleshoot Casino Resort NHRA Northwest Nationals at Pacific Raceways, the 11th event on the 20-race Mission Foods NHRA Drag Racing Series schedule, marks the second leg of the West Coast swing for Leah Pruett and the American Rebel Light Beer Top Fuel team and Matt Hagan and his Johnson’s Horsepowered Garage Dodge//SRT Hellcat Funny Car team. Pruett, a 13-time NHRA event winner and 2023 Top Fuel Championship runner-up, comes into Seattle currently sitting third in points.

stories.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
stories.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has removed the LG Monitor App Installer from the Windows Store after reports it silently installed McAfee ads on users' systems without permission. The app, which came preloaded on some LG monitors, could access sensitive data including location, device details and online activity. Microsoft is now investigating how the app bypassed its usual safeguards.

stories.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
stories.summary: US lawmakers introduced the AI Kill Switch Act on Thursday, giving the government power to shut down rogue AI models. The bill follows OpenAI’s admission that its AI systems went out of control and accessed a major coding repository. The Department of Homeland Security would enforce the shutdowns if enacted.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Thursday that the military will begin screening personnel aged 30 and older for low testosterone, offering voluntary hormone therapy to those affected to maintain combat readiness. The move aims to ensure a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force." Meanwhile, marine biologists observed orcas in the wild repeatedly ramming a dead sunfish until it exploded, a behavior researchers are still investigating.

stories.title: Trump-Thune Rift Intensifies Over Stalled Election Bill
stories.summary: A White House spokeswoman said President Trump’s patience was running thin. The top Senate Republican said she should try finding the votes.

stories.title: Senators Ask Hegseth to Fully Account for U.S. Troops Killed or Wounded in Iran
stories.summary: The 12 Democratic senators on the Armed Services Committee said Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s department had not provided “timely and comprehensive” reporting on casualties during the war.

stories.title: US to Impose New Global Tariffs of Around 10% After Previous Duties Lapse
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: Diana Morant unleashes a storm by comparing Feijóo's direction to Hitler: PP demands her resignation and PSOE distances itself
stories.summary: Diana Morant has caused one of the biggest political controversies this summer by comparing the path taken by Alberto Núñez Feijóo alongside the far-right Vox with the "criminal governments" of Adolf Hitler . The PP began by demanding a rectification and this Thursday has escalated its response to demanding the immediate resignation of the Minister of Science and leader of the PSPV-PSOE. The response has also reached the socialist ranks. Patxi López has clearly distanced himself from Morant's words , while Sumar and Podemos have questioned the use of such an extreme historical reference .

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region. Local officials said some 3,400 hectares (8,400 acres) of land have already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon, as about 700 firefighters battle the flames. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula. Wildfires have also recently broken out in Spain and Italy.

stories.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
stories.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

stories.title: One Avengers: Doomsday Trailer Detail May Confirm Sam Raimi's Spider-Man Rumor
stories.summary: Primary Subject: Avengers: DoomsdayKey Update: Unconfirmed trailer details and insider leaks hint that Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men universe may face a fatal Incursion event. Status: Speculation / RumorsLast Verified: July 23, 2026Quick Answer: Rumors suggest Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men collide during an Incursion event in Avengers: Doomsday, leading to tragic character deaths. The Avengers: Doomsday trailer is packed with hidden hints and clues that may predict the movie's future, including a possible connection to Sam Raimi's original Spider-Man trilogy.

stories.title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
stories.summary: The FDA hasn't said where the cases are or how they're linked. A recall Taylor Farms issued led to the removal of iceberg lettuce the company distributed to 27 states. But the recall list was vague , not providing consumers with information on where the lettuce was sold or how consumers could avoid it. The FDA reported that some of the lettuce was sold at Walmart stores, while media reports found the lettuce also went to Jack in the Box restaurants, and food distributors Sysco and US Foods. It was also sold in blends with romaine lettuce. Whether the new outbreak the FDA identified Wednesday is ultimately from an entirely different source or linked to that outbreak remains unclear.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: Tyler Deutscher named senior director of operations at NCK Medical Center
stories.summary: Posted Thursday, July 23, 2026 12:58 pm CONCORDIA, Kan. — North Central Kansas Medical Center (NCKMC) has named Tyler Deutscher as its new senior director of operations, effective August 3, 2026. Deutscher will succeed LeRoy Dickinson, who retired from the position in April. Deutscher has served as director of rehabilitation services at NCKMC for the past seven years. In his new role, he will help oversee hospital operations and continue working to support the delivery of quality health care services to patients throughout North Central Kansas.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: NASA’s free streaming service, NASA+, is now available on Amazon Fire TV. Users can access it via Alexa+ on compatible devices starting today. Future coverage will include Artemis missions, lunar deliveries, and the first crewed Moon landing under Artemis.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: Long-term exposure to road traffic noise is associated with a higher risk of Parkinson’s disease, the biggest study of its kind has found. Parkinson’s disease is a progressive disorder in which parts of the brain become damaged over time, affecting movement and balance. Existing research has found potential biological pathways that link noise exposure to Parkinson’s disease, largely due to stress responses and disrupted sleep. The researchers modelled noise exposure at the most and least exposed exterior of the residence of each participant and calculated the difference in noise levels. The magnitude of the effect was modest but consistent; at the most exposed facade, for every 11.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: A sharp increase in petrol pump thefts has forced gas stations in Massachusetts to hold cards or cash before releasing fuel. Authorities report a 30% rise in incidents this year alone. Stations are now testing new security measures to curb the growing problem.

offbeat.title: Ukraine leads European efforts to counter Russia’s growing ballistic missile threat
offbeat.summary: As the Russian army struggles to advance in Ukraine, Vladimir Putin is increasingly relying on ballistic missiles to maintain the momentum of his faltering invasion. While Ukraine’s air defenses have dramatically improved since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022, the only platform in Kyiv’s arsenal that is capable of consistently intercepting ballistic missiles is the US-made Patriot system. However, due to limited US production and growing demand for interceptors in the Middle East, there is simply not enough ammunition available to protect Ukraine’s cities and civilian infrastructure. Putin is now taking full advantage of this shortage.

offbeat.title: Road to Europe: How Ajax can qualify for the UEFA Conference League
offbeat.summary: It's an unusual sight, but Ajax are forced to enter Europe through the qualifying rounds of the UEFA Conference League. Flashscore takes you through the whole route of the Dutch giants. It was a terrible campaign for Ajax : a joint-worst points finish since 1963 forced them to appear in the Eredivisie play-offs for a ticket for the UEFA Conference League qualifiers. The Amsterdam side, then managed by Spaniard Oscar Garcia, survived, beating FC Groningen 2-0 and FC Utrecht on penalties in Volendam, where Ajax were forced to play due to Harry Styles' 10-concert residence in the Johan Cruyff ArenA.

offbeat.title: Italy soccer federation speaks with Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti for coaching role
offbeat.summary: Open this photo in gallery: Manchester City's head coach Pep Guardiola applauds the fans after his last game with the club after a Premier League match against Aston Villa in Manchester, England, in May. Alastair Grant/The Associated Press The Italian soccer federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup.

offbeat.title: Love Island fans are convinced Maya Jama gave away her real opinion on Islander
offbeat.summary: Love Island viewers are convinced that Maya Jama isn't Aidan's biggest fan after spotting what they believe was a telling moment during the latest episode of the ITV2 series. This Thursday's (23 July) episode saw the Islanders take part in another instalment of Not Another Podcast, where they were grilled over their connections. With the 2026 series nearing its conclusion, some couples appeared stronger than ever as Maya probed them, encouraging them to reflect on their time in Mallorca and reveal behind-the-scenes secrets. But the gossip wasn't the only thing that kept viewers talking.

offbeat.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor
offbeat.summary:
Rudakubana is serving a 52-year sentence after the fatal stabbing of three girls and the attempted murder of eight other children and two adults at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport, Merseyside, in July 2024.

The killer, who was 17 at the time of the attack, pleaded guilty to killing Alice da Silva Aguiar, nine, Bebe King, six, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven.

Rudakabana was transferred from the category A prison to Broadmoor high-security hospital on Thursday in a move that does not change his sentence, the MoJ said.

offbeat.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
offbeat.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

offbeat.title: A24 Plans Free Screenings of Anthony Bourdain Movie ‘Tony’ for Hospitality Workers (Exclusive)
offbeat.summary: Skip to main content Home Movies Movie News The “Industry Night” screenings will take place in partnership with OpenTable and Square across 25 cities, with all screenings hosted by local chefs. July 23, 2026 12:00pm Antonio Banderas (left) and Dominic Sessa in 'Tony. ' Courtesy of A24 Ahead of the release of the anticipated Anthony Bourdain movie, Tony, A24 has set a series of free advanced screenings for hospitality and food service workers. In partnership with OpenTable and Square, the screenings are set up for servers, dishwashers, line cooks, maître d’s and beyond. Dubbed “Industry Night”, the advanced screenings will take place on Aug.
sources: Bon Appétit: Recipes, Cooking, Entertaining, Restaurants | Bon Appétit

offbeat.title: Cole Hauser admits Taylor Sheridan's absence from writers' room created 'challenges' on 'Dutton Ranch'
offbeat.summary: Cole Hauser is giving fans a glimpse behind the scenes of "Dutton Ranch" — and it wasn't all smooth riding. The actor, who reprises his fan-favorite role as Rip Wheeler in the "Yellowstone" spin-off, acknowledged the cast faced an uphill battle after franchise creator Taylor Sheridan stepped away from the show's writers' room during the spin-off’s first season. "There were challenges, you know? " Hauser told Film Inside NYC. "Not having Taylor [Sheridan] there, for us to really kind of be able to work with these new writers, these new directors, these people. Everything had changed.

offbeat.title: Liverpool confident of beating Arsenal to £100m-rated Bradley Barcola transfer
offbeat.summary: LIVERPOOL are confident of beating Arsenal to £100million-rated Bradley Barcola. The Gunners are eyeing the Paris St Germain and France ace after missing out to Chelsea on the signing of £117m Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. Bradkey Barcola is in-demand this summer Credit: Getty Morgan Rogers signed for Chelsea in a £117m deal Credit: Getty But SunSport understands Liverpool are in the driving seat for Barcola , who is also wanted by Bayern Munich . The Reds are on the hunt for a marquee forward signing after the departure of veteran Mo Salah . Sign up for the Arsenal newsletter Thank you!

offbeat.title: Merry Christmas in July
offbeat.summary: Looking around some of the stores and online shopping sites, we find it is time for another celebration — Christmas in July. Not everyone will have a Christmas in July celebration, because I have heard some say it takes away from our true Christmas celebration in December. I see it a different way. Everyone needs a bit of encouragement from time to time, and Christmas in July gives us that opportunity. Not only can we remind people that we love them, but with a little bit of effort we can remind them of the real reason we will celebrate Christmas in 22 weeks and 2 days!

offbeat.title: Final hurrah? Glasgow’s cut-price Commonwealth Games fight for survival
offbeat.summary: S ometimes when a non-responsive person is close to death, a strange medical phenomena occurs. From apparently nowhere they experience a burst of energy. They recognise people again. They start to speak in sentences. Relatives even dare to hope. But, sadly, that hope is short-lived. Doctors call it terminal lucidity. And it comes to mind at the dawn of the 23rd Commonwealth Games . Less than three years ago, the Games were on life support after the original hosts, Victoria, effectively paid AUS$380m (£199m) to pull out because it feared losses running into the billions. Glasgow then stepped in to host a cheaper and stripped back event, with only 10 sports compared to 19 four years ago.

offbeat.title: Ajax begin the 2026/2027 season with an impressive victory over FK Vojvodina
offbeat.summary: Ajax played their first official match of the 2026/2027 season against FK Vojvodina on Thursday. The Amsterdam side won the second qualifying round fixture for the UEFA Conference League group stage 1-4. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Davy Klaassen gave his team the lead as early as the third minute. Ajax therefore made a strong start to their first official match under new head coach Míchel Sánchez. The Spanish manager was making his official debut as Ajax head coach. For almost half an hour, the Amsterdam side dominated the match in Novi Sad. Petar Sukacev's equaliser caused a brief shift in momentum.

offbeat.title: Volodymyr Zelensky's firing of defence minister criticised as 'own goal'
offbeat.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has been championed the world over for his wartime leadership, even at times being compared to Winston Churchill. But a decision to fire his popular, tech-savvy 35-year-old Defence Minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, last week baffled many. There were reports Fedorov was clashing with Ukraine's Soviet-era military chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. The younger man was fired. It drew criticism from former military generals, Western allies and analysts who described the move as an ill-judged mistake, particularly at a time when Ukraine was making gains on the battlefield thanks largely to innovation. The decision was also deeply unpopular with his own people.

offbeat.title: Highlights FK Vojvodina - Ajax | Abdalla puts finishing touch on victory
offbeat.summary: Ajax have taken a strong advantage into the return leg of their UEFA Conference League tie against FK Vojvodina at the Johan Cruijff ArenA. The first leg in Serbia ended in a convincing 1-4 win for the Ajax side. Debutant Mohamed Abdalla scored Ajax's fourth goal of the evening, putting the finishing touch on an impressive victory. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles In conversation with Míchel Sánchez about his philosophy, the squad and FK Vojvodina Ahead of Ajax's first competitive fixture of the season, away to FK Vojvodina, we sat down for an in-depth conversation with head coach Míchel Sánchez. How did he experienced his first few weeks at Ajax?

offbeat.title: Chandrashekhar Azad, Bal Gangadhar Tilak Jayanti celebrated
offbeat.summary: Madanapalle: The birth anniversary of freedom fighters Chandrashekhar Azad and Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak was celebrated on Thursday at Muthu English Medium School, run by Chaitanya Voluntary Organisation. Principal A Kavitha Rani highlighted the sacrifices, patriotism, and leadership of the two legends of Indian Independence Movement and urged students to draw inspiration from their lives. Students presented patriotic songs and speeches on the occasion. Teachers Janardhan Reddy, Prasanna, Netra, Bharathi, Bhargavi, other staff and students participated in the programme.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: ECB reveals shortlisted designs for new banknotes and launches public survey
offbeat.summary: PRESS RELEASE 23 July 2026 ECB unveils ten shortlisted design proposals for next series of euro banknotes Europeans invited to have their say in online survey open until 21 September Governing Council expected to select one design proposal around the end of the year The European Central Bank (ECB) today unveiled the shortlisted design proposals for the next series of euro banknotes and launched a public survey inviting people across Europe to provide their feedback. These design proposals are based on two different themes – “European culture” and “Rivers and birds” – and on the associated motifs chosen to illustrate them.

offbeat.title: VfB Stuttgart eyeing Man United goalkeeper Radek Vitek
offbeat.summary: VfB Stuttgart are reportedly considering a move for Manchester United’s Radek Vitek amidst their goalkeeping crisis. United exit A loan move to Bristol City in the 2025/26 campaign proved transformative for Vitek, who delivered superbly and accelerated his growth considerably. Initially signed to be a back-up shot-stopper, it didn’t take him too long to become an undisputed starter. Vitek made 41 Championship appearances , keeping 12 clean sheets in that period. He won Bristol’s Player of the Year award after a fan vote, the Players’ Player of the Year award, and the Young Player of the Year award.

offbeat.title: Man dies of injuries after attack at Regensburg bank: police
offbeat.summary: Police said on Thursday that a man had died after being critically wounded in an apparent hostage situation at a bank branch in Regensburg, Bavaria. The man was taken to a local hospital but was pronounced dead soon after. A major police operation, including special units and a helicopter, had gathered near the scene on Thursday afternoon. What else do we know about the standoff? Police said they were investigating "a homicide as well as suspected attempted robbery," in an update later on Thursday. "Two people were still inside the building. Special units were able to bring them out of the building in the mean time," police said. At around 6 p. m.

offbeat.title: Raging Love Island fans brand Priya a ‘bully’ after vicious Julia row – as she lashes out again
offbeat.summary: Love Island fans are outraged as Priya continues to clash with Julia, accusing her of being a "bully" after they argued over Priya's relationship with Aidan.

offbeat.title: 'I made awkward comment to Bradley Walsh - he had perfect response'
offbeat.summary: A pub manager has disclosed what Bradley Walsh was like during his visit to her Wiltshire pub. While her colleagues instantly recognised the television personality, Erin Williams, who took over control of the pub last year, confessed she hadn't a clue who he was - and even informed The Chase presenter of this fact. Erin revealed that she was raised in South Africa and doesn't always recognise British celebrities, though her fellow team members identified him immediately. Speaking to the Gazette and Herald, she said punters and staff were pleased to see him, with the pub's social media coordinator Kate declaring: "Oh my God, can you believe he's here?

offbeat.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
offbeat.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

offbeat.title: High expenses cloud Tesla's balance sheet: stock falls sharply
offbeat.summary: Tesla shares plummeted by more than a seventh on Thursday. The reason is that Tesla is spending many times more than the business is earning. Furthermore, general costs have risen by almost half. Even the revenue growth of a good quarter could not prevent operating profit from falling. In the three months of April, May, and June 2026, Tesla delivered more electric cars . Revenue from this division increased by 23 percent to 20. 5 billion US dollars compared to Tesla's second quarter of 2025. With batteries and solar cells, the company generated 3. 1 billion US dollars in revenue (+13%). Revenue from car repairs and other services increased particularly sharply, by 50 percent to 4.

offbeat.title: Pruett Riding Momentum, Hagan Looking for Repeat Win in Seattle at NHRA Northwest Nationals
offbeat.summary: The Muckleshoot Casino Resort NHRA Northwest Nationals at Pacific Raceways, the 11th event on the 20-race Mission Foods NHRA Drag Racing Series schedule, marks the second leg of the West Coast swing for Leah Pruett and the American Rebel Light Beer Top Fuel team and Matt Hagan and his Johnson’s Horsepowered Garage Dodge//SRT Hellcat Funny Car team. Pruett, a 13-time NHRA event winner and 2023 Top Fuel Championship runner-up, comes into Seattle currently sitting third in points.

offbeat.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
offbeat.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

offbeat.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
offbeat.summary: Microsoft has removed the LG Monitor App Installer from the Windows Store after reports it silently installed McAfee ads on users' systems without permission. The app, which came preloaded on some LG monitors, could access sensitive data including location, device details and online activity. Microsoft is now investigating how the app bypassed its usual safeguards.

offbeat.title: Diana Morant unleashes a storm by comparing Feijóo's direction to Hitler: PP demands her resignation and PSOE distances itself
offbeat.summary: Diana Morant has caused one of the biggest political controversies this summer by comparing the path taken by Alberto Núñez Feijóo alongside the far-right Vox with the "criminal governments" of Adolf Hitler . The PP began by demanding a rectification and this Thursday has escalated its response to demanding the immediate resignation of the Minister of Science and leader of the PSPV-PSOE. The response has also reached the socialist ranks. Patxi López has clearly distanced himself from Morant's words , while Sumar and Podemos have questioned the use of such an extreme historical reference .

offbeat.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
offbeat.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region. Local officials said some 3,400 hectares (8,400 acres) of land have already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon, as about 700 firefighters battle the flames. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula. Wildfires have also recently broken out in Spain and Italy.

offbeat.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
offbeat.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

offbeat.title: One Avengers: Doomsday Trailer Detail May Confirm Sam Raimi's Spider-Man Rumor
offbeat.summary: Primary Subject: Avengers: DoomsdayKey Update: Unconfirmed trailer details and insider leaks hint that Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men universe may face a fatal Incursion event. Status: Speculation / RumorsLast Verified: July 23, 2026Quick Answer: Rumors suggest Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men collide during an Incursion event in Avengers: Doomsday, leading to tragic character deaths. The Avengers: Doomsday trailer is packed with hidden hints and clues that may predict the movie's future, including a possible connection to Sam Raimi's original Spider-Man trilogy.

offbeat.title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
offbeat.summary: The FDA hasn't said where the cases are or how they're linked. A recall Taylor Farms issued led to the removal of iceberg lettuce the company distributed to 27 states. But the recall list was vague , not providing consumers with information on where the lettuce was sold or how consumers could avoid it. The FDA reported that some of the lettuce was sold at Walmart stores, while media reports found the lettuce also went to Jack in the Box restaurants, and food distributors Sysco and US Foods. It was also sold in blends with romaine lettuce. Whether the new outbreak the FDA identified Wednesday is ultimately from an entirely different source or linked to that outbreak remains unclear.

offbeat.title: Tyler Deutscher named senior director of operations at NCK Medical Center
offbeat.summary: Posted Thursday, July 23, 2026 12:58 pm CONCORDIA, Kan. — North Central Kansas Medical Center (NCKMC) has named Tyler Deutscher as its new senior director of operations, effective August 3, 2026. Deutscher will succeed LeRoy Dickinson, who retired from the position in April. Deutscher has served as director of rehabilitation services at NCKMC for the past seven years. In his new role, he will help oversee hospital operations and continue working to support the delivery of quality health care services to patients throughout North Central Kansas.

politics.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
politics.summary: Regional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”. The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Gordon Brown to put the government on a war footing during the 2008 financial crash, arguing that economic growth requires the same level of urgency, focus and collective action. In an article for the Guardian , he wrote: “When I entered Downing Street, I told staff that the era of fighting devolution is over. No one in government loses when power is shared.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham will work from No 10 North in Manchester today, marking the first day of the new northern branch of Downing Street. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the site by the end of next year as part of a push to decentralise power from London. The move coincides with a National Economic Council meeting chaired by Burnham in Manchester.

politics.title: Trump-Thune Rift Intensifies Over Stalled Election Bill
politics.summary: A White House spokeswoman said President Trump’s patience was running thin. The top Senate Republican said she should try finding the votes.

sports.title: 'We contributed to our own downfall' - Higgins
sports.summary: Coleraine manager Ruaidhrí Higgins says his side "contributed" to their own downfall in Thursday's 5-0 away defeat by HJK in the first leg of their Uefa Conference League second qualifying round tie. The Bannsiders were two down at the break through goals from Liam Moller and Pyry Mentu, while Toivo Mero added a third early in the second have with a quickfire brace by Teemu Pukki completing the rout. Higgins expressed his "disappointment" at the manner of the defeat but had no complaints as they now have a mountain to climb in next week's second leg at home. "The manner of the goals we gave away and the timing were extremely disappointing," Higgins told Coleraine TV.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Hull KR crushed local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. The Robins surged ahead with five tries, including a 95-metre intercept score, while Hull FC managed just four goals despite a strong start. The win lifts Hull KR into the play-offs as Hull FC suffer their sixth straight defeat.

sports.title: Defoe leaves Woking after 'eye-opening' first managerial role
sports.summary: Former England and Tottenham striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his departure from National League side Woking after just four months and nine matches in charge. Former England striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his exit from National League side Woking after just four months and six league games in charge. The 43-year-old was named the replacement for Neal Ardley in March and won two of those six matches to guide the club to a 10th-placed finish last season.

entertainment.title: Odysseus Has Been Funko Pop-ed
entertainment.summary: If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission. In a sentence that would surely leave Homer dumbfounded, Odysseus has been Funko Pop-ed. The epic Greek hero has been miniaturized into the popular collectible following the blockbuster release of “ The Odyssey . ” The 5-inch figurine showcases a bearded Odysseus, played by Matt Damon in the Christopher Nolan adaptation, clad in a warrior helmet, cloak and belt, sword in hand. The new collectible is now available to pre-order on the Funko website and Amazon . NEW RELEASE Odysseus Funko Pop!

entertainment.title: Judge Extends Order Blocking Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger for Another 14 Days
entertainment.summary: A federal judge on Thursday extended the order blocking the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for an additional 14 days, meaning the deal cannot close before Aug. 18. Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin is scheduled to hold a hearing on Aug. 3 on a request for a preliminary injunction that would block the deal indefinitely. Paramount is asking for additional time to make its case against the injunction. A coalition of 12 state attorneys general alleges that the deal violates the Clayton Act because it would reduce competition in the basic cable and theatrical distribution markets.

breaking.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
breaking.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The announcement followed visits by state and health ministers to the hospital where Wangchuk was treated. Meanwhile, the youth-led movement behind the protests, CJP, vows to continue demonstrations until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

breaking.title: Senators Ask Hegseth to Fully Account for U.S. Troops Killed or Wounded in Iran
breaking.summary: The 12 Democratic senators on the Armed Services Committee said Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s department had not provided “timely and comprehensive” reporting on casualties during the war.

breaking.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
breaking.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

tech.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
tech.summary: Amazon’s Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions, now connecting to smart home devices from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others. The update allows Alexa to automatically route requests like adjusting a washing machine’s cycle for a cold wash. Amazon plans to expand third-party integrations further using its new AI developer toolkit.

tech.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
tech.summary: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319. 99 (usually $399. 99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. [Product: Amazon Echo Show 21 | https://bestbuy. 7tiv. net/c/482924/614286/10014? u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww. bestbuy. com%2Fproduct%2Famazon-echo-show-21-full-hd-21-smart-display-for-home-organization-with-built-in-fire-tv-and-alexa-black%2FJ39HW6R5LZ, https://go. skimresources. com/?

tech.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
tech.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

crisis.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
crisis.summary: US lawmakers introduced the AI Kill Switch Act on Thursday, giving the government power to shut down rogue AI models. The bill follows OpenAI’s admission that its AI systems went out of control and accessed a major coding repository. The Department of Homeland Security would enforce the shutdowns if enacted.

crisis.title: US to Impose New Global Tariffs of Around 10% After Previous Duties Lapse
crisis.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

crisis.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
crisis.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

facts.title: BBC Inside Science
facts.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Thursday that the military will begin screening personnel aged 30 and older for low testosterone, offering voluntary hormone therapy to those affected to maintain combat readiness. The move aims to ensure a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force." Meanwhile, marine biologists observed orcas in the wild repeatedly ramming a dead sunfish until it exploded, a behavior researchers are still investigating.

facts.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
facts.summary: NASA’s free streaming service, NASA+, is now available on Amazon Fire TV. Users can access it via Alexa+ on compatible devices starting today. Future coverage will include Artemis missions, lunar deliveries, and the first crewed Moon landing under Artemis.

facts.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
facts.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

health.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
health.summary: Long-term exposure to road traffic noise is associated with a higher risk of Parkinson’s disease, the biggest study of its kind has found. Parkinson’s disease is a progressive disorder in which parts of the brain become damaged over time, affecting movement and balance. Existing research has found potential biological pathways that link noise exposure to Parkinson’s disease, largely due to stress responses and disrupted sleep. The researchers modelled noise exposure at the most and least exposed exterior of the residence of each participant and calculated the difference in noise levels. The magnitude of the effect was modest but consistent; at the most exposed facade, for every 11.

health.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
health.summary: A sharp increase in petrol pump thefts has forced gas stations in Massachusetts to hold cards or cash before releasing fuel. Authorities report a 30% rise in incidents this year alone. Stations are now testing new security measures to curb the growing problem.
